title: SetResponse<T = serde_json::Value> default type parameter avoids noise at call sites
description: SetResponse uses a default type parameter (T = serde_json::Value). This lets methods that don't need a typed created/updated map write SetResponse in their return type without a type argument, while methods that do need typed objects can specialize. The alternative — always requiring an explicit type argument — adds visual noise to every call site and return type annotation. The alternative of separate typed/untyped structs would duplicate field definitions. The default parameter is idiomatic Rust for exactly this situation and should not be removed in favor of either alternative.
